L1.area: Calendar event data retrieval and API service layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled HTTP request parameters are directly interpolated into HQL query strings without sanitization or parameterized binding. One parameter allows specifying an entirely custom query string, bypassing any intended query structure. An attacker with view access, including unauthenticated guest users, can inject arbitrary query language to extract database information or perform denial of service attacks.

L3.area: Calendar JSON data service endpoints responsible for querying and returning event data
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled HTTP request parameters, including one that allows specifying an entirely custom query string and another used for class name filtering, are directly interpolated into HQL query strings without parameterized binding in the calendar's JSON data service. An attacker with view access, including unauthenticated guest users, can inject arbitrary HQL to extract database information or perform denial of service attacks.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled HTTP request parameters are directly interpolated into HQL query strings in the calendar JSON service, allowing unauthenticated SQL injection that can leak database contents or cause denial of service.
